title: Scandinavian Defense: Modern
summary: Learn the aggressive Scandinavian where Black captures and holds the center, creating unique tactical opportunities.
description: The Scandinavian is a tricky opening where Black captures on e4 (or d4) and tries to hold the pawn. You'll learn why it's dangerous for Black if they don't handle it correctly, and how White can punish greedy play.
objectives: Understand why capturing on e4 is risky: Black must develop quickly or lose the pawn.
Learn typical White responses: develop, attack the queen, and punish slow development.
Master the key idea: don't let Black develop in peace.
plans: White uses the extra tempo to develop and attack the queen.
If Black holds the pawn, White attacks it with piece pressure.
The Scandinavian is sharp: accurate play from either side decides.
commonMistakes: Do not play passively. White has a lead in development.
Avoid letting Black develop peacefully. Keep the pressure on.
Do not underestimate Black's counterplay. The open queen file is dangerous.
completionSummary: The Scandinavian teaches that capturing in front can be dangerous if development lags.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.coaching: The king pawn opening. White controls the center and invites the Scandinavian response.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: The Scandinavian setup
steps.instruction: Play d5 to challenge e4 immediately.
steps.coaching: This is the Scandinavian: Black attacks e4 before developing pieces.

steps.title: Capture the pawn
steps.instruction: Take on d5, opening the game.
steps.coaching: White accepts the pawn. Now Black must develop or lose it.
steps.hint: Capture on d5 with the pawn.

steps.title: Black captures greedily
steps.instruction: The queen takes, holding the pawn but lagging in development.
steps.coaching: This is the main line: Black holds the pawn but the queen is exposed.

steps.title: Develop and attack
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing and attacking the queen.
steps.coaching: The knight attacks the queen and develops. White has the lead in development.

steps.title: Black moves the queen
steps.instruction: The queen moves to safety, developing.
steps.coaching: The queen moves to a5, developing. But White continues to develop.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center and prepare development.
steps.coaching: White claims space while Black's queen is exposed.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to f6, attacking e4.
steps.coaching: Black continues development. The position is now complex.

steps.title: Complete development
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, threatening the queen's retreat.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.instruction: The bishop develops to g4, pinning the knight.
steps.coaching: Black pins and develops. The position is now balanced but complex.
